summary: PR 2378 linked the ftCommon fttpickupitem object by marking it Matching, adding a small inline accessor for Fighter.x294_itPickup used by fttpickupitem_800942A0, and adjusting symbols for local strings/floats and a local data label. The bot report records one new match: main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftpickupitem fttpickupitem_800942A0 reaching 100.00%.

key_files.role: Source change: added inline itPickup accessor fttpickupitem_800942A0_inline(Fighter* fp) returning &fp->x294_itPickup, then used it in fttpickupitem_800942A0.
Build/config change: changed melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftpickupitem.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
Symbol metadata change: renamed several ftpickupitem-associated .data and .sdata2 entries from semantic/global-looking names to local @ labels and added a local ...data.0 label at 0x803C5580.

what_changed.text: Changed `ftpickupitem_800942A0` to initialize `pickup` through the new inline helper instead of directly taking `&fp->x294_itPickup`.

Adjusted `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` entries around `.data:0x803C5580` through `.data:0x803C55B4`, replacing `ftItemPickup_803C5580`, `assert_msg1`, and `assert_msg2` with local `@273`, `@274`, `@275` labels and adding local label `...data.0`.
Adjusted `.sdata2` entries at `0x804D8580`, `0x804D8584`, and `0x804D8588` from global `ftCo_804D8580/8584/8588` names to local `@253`, `@293`, and `@294` float labels.
Bot report recorded matched code +476 bytes, linked code +3332 bytes, linked data +80 bytes, and one new match: `ftpickupitem_800942A0` from 99.54% to 100.00%.

smart_moves.text: Kept the source change narrow: only introduced a small inline accessor and changed one assignment in the matched function before linking the whole object as Matching.

Updated symbol metadata in the same PR as object linking, including localizing nearby string and float symbols and adding a local data label where the diff indicates it was needed for the linked object.

decomp_lessons.lesson: A trivial inline accessor for a struct subobject can be a matching tactic when direct field address-taking produces the wrong code shape; in this PR, routing `&fp->x294_itPickup` through `ftpickupitem_800942A0_inline` coincided with `ftpickupitem_800942A0` reaching 100.00%.

Object-linking PRs may require symbol metadata cleanup alongside source changes; here the linked object change included local string/float labels and a local `.data` label in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`.

naming_conventions.text: The new helper name follows the local address-style function naming pattern with a descriptive `_inline` suffix: `ftpickupitem_800942A0_inline`.
Symbol file changes prefer anonymous/local labels (`@273`, `@274`, `@275`, `@253`, `@293`, `@294`) over the previous global-looking names for these local strings/floats.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a narrow inline helper to alter or preserve MWCC codegen for taking the address of a fighter substructure.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR evidence shows the helper was added and the bot reported a match, but does not include objdiff details proving this helper alone caused the match.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Flip the object from NonMatching to Matching only after the bot report shows `ftpickupitem_800942A0` at 100.00% and linked-code progress.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The report is a PR bot summary, not a full local build/objdiff ledger.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Correct symbol scope/name metadata for data and sdata2 entries near the linked object, changing several apparent globals to local labels.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The slice does not include explanatory comments tying each metadata change to a specific relocation or diff requirement.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 showed matched code 61.71% (+0.01%, +476 bytes), linked code 33.88% (+0.09%, +3332 bytes), linked data 32.34% (+0.01%, +80 bytes), and one new match for `main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftpickupitem` item `ftpickupitem_800942A0` from 99.54% to 100.00%.

notes: The diff and bot report are complete enough to identify files changed, concrete source edits, symbol metadata edits, and reported match progress.
The PR body is empty and there are no human review comments in the loaded slice.

The bot report supports the claim of one new match for `ftpickupitem_800942A0`, but the slice does not include full objdiff output, local build logs, or author explanation for why the inline helper and symbol changes were necessary.
Merged state and timestamps are present in metadata, but merge acceptance should not be interpreted as a general standard beyond this PR.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Tiny inline subobject accessor can unblock a function match
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2378, `ftpickupitem_800942A0` changed from directly assigning `pickup = &fp->x294_itPickup;` to assigning through a newly added inline helper returning the same address. The bot report then recorded `ftpickupitem_800942A0` at 100.00%. Preserve as a narrow matching tactic candidate, not a blanket style rule.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Linking a matched object can require symbol metadata locality fixes
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2378 linked `ftpickupitem.c` and also changed several `.data`/`.sdata2` entries in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` from named global-looking symbols to local labels, plus added a local data label. Treat as evidence that object-linking work may include symbol scope/name cleanup when section metadata requires it.

source_update_candidates.title: Document inline helper as a narrow matching tactic only with verifier evidence
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a note under matching tactics that introducing a tiny inline helper for an otherwise equivalent field/subobject access can be valid when supported by objdiff/build evidence, but should remain narrow and should not obscure real types. PR 2378 is a supporting example: `ftpickupitem_800942A0_inline` returned `&fp->x294_itPickup` and the bot reported the target function as newly 100.00%.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards discuss matching tactics needing evidence, but do not explicitly mention equivalent inline accessor helpers as a possible narrow tactic.

source_update_candidates.title: ftCommon fttpickupitem linked as Matching in PR 2378
source_update_candidates.text: `melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftpickupitem.c` was changed from `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to `Object(Matching, ...)` in `configure.py`; bot report listed one new match for `main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftpickupitem` item `ftpickupitem_800942A0` at 100.00%.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-level historical fact for future searches about ftCommon matching progress.

source_update_candidates.title: ftpickupitem symbol metadata used local labels for strings/floats
source_update_candidates.text: In PR 2378, `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` changed `ftItemPickup_803C5580`, `assert_msg1`, `assert_msg2`, and `ftCo_804D8580/8584/8588` to local `@` labels, and added local label `...data.0` at `.data:0x803C5580`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future curation of data/symbol ownership around the linked fttpickupitem object.

rejection_notes: Do not promote a global rule that direct field address-taking should be replaced by inline helpers; this PR only supports one narrow matching instance.
Do not infer human reviewer intent or rationale; the slice has no review comments and an empty PR body.
Do not claim full local validation beyond the bot report; no full build log or objdiff details are present in the loaded evidence.
Do not treat the symbol renames as proof of semantic meaning for the affected strings/floats; the diff only proves metadata/name/scope changes.
